Article Title: The art of win-win
Article Text: Thirukkural with the Times explores real-world lessons from the classic Tamil text ‘Thirukkural’. Written by Tamil poet and philosopher Thiruvalluvar, the Kural consists of 1,330 short couplets of seven words each. This text is divided into three books with teachings on virtue, wealth, and love and is considered one of the great works ever on ethics and morality. The Kural has influenced scholars and leaders across social, political, and philosophical spheres. Motivational speaker, author and diversity champion Bharathi Bhaskar explores the masterpiece. Even after weeks of heightened tensions and back-channel diplomacy , the recent US-Iran negotiations failed to produce a lasting break-through. Once again, the two sides walked away without agreement; in negotiations, talking is not the same as reaching common ground. How to achieve success in negotiations is a key question today, in politics, business and relationships. When I was a child, every Sunday afternoon, the stainless steel vessel vendor would visit our street, balancing a giant wicker basket filled with gleaming vessels on his head. My mother would lift one of the larger vessels, weigh it in her hands and ask, "How much?" The vendor never answered immediately. "For others, it is one price. For you, Amma, I will give a special rate, Rs 30." "Who will pay 30 for something this flimsy? It is worth no more than three rupees," she would reply. As a child, I would want to disappear. Three rupees? Surely the man would be offended, shout at us and walk away. Instead, the conversation would continue with complete composure until the Rs30 vessel changed hands for around Rs17. Before the vendor lifted the basket again, my mother would insist that he drank a large tumbler of chilled buttermilk, before setting out in the afternoon heat. Only years later did I realise that those afternoons had taught me my first lessons in negotiation . The first lesson was patience. Good negotiators are willing to wait. They know that time itself can become part of the negotiation. The second lesson is that negotiation is rarely won by the loudest voices. Those who dominate conversations often reveal more than they realise. Skilled negotiators speak little, listen carefully and are comfortable with silence. A thoughtful "Hmm..." followed by a pause can achieve far more than a torrent of arguments. Silence has remarkable persuasive power. One of the best ways to negotiate is to let the other person talk and make them feel they are steering the conversation. Instead of imposing solutions, they gently guide the other side towards discovering them. People are far more committed to an idea they believe is their own. The third lesson is to negotiate only with the person who is the decision maker. There is a delightful moment in the Valmiki Ramayana. When Hanuman is brought before Ravana, a minister of Ravana’s begins to address him. "Oh, monkey, do not fear. Speak the truth and I shall request the king to spare you," he says. Hanuman does not even acknowledge the minister and speaks directly to Ravana. It is a subtle but profound lesson. Do not spend your energy negotiating with people who have no authority. The final lesson is that the best negotiations leaving both parties believing they have gained something worthwhile. My mother would sometimes sweeten the bargain by adding an old sari with gold zari along with the money. The vendor left smiling, and so did she. It is this spirit that Thirukkural captures in Kural 679: Nattarkku nalla seyalin viraindhadhe Ottarai ottik kolal. "Just as you strive to get the best for your people, ensure that even your opponents receive something and are not estranged." The finest negotiation is never about creating a solitary winner. It is about building a bridge across differences, where everyone walks away beaming. I wish the world leaders had seen their mothers negotiating with street vendors. They might have picked up some important lessons.
Article Summary: Bharathi Bhaskar explores real-world lessons from the classic Tamil text 'Thirukkural' Written by Tamil poet and philosopher Thiruvalluvar, the Kural consists of 1,330 short couplets of seven words each.

Article Title: Brain, heart, lungs missing: MEA seeks urgent probe by Venezuela in Indian sailor's death
Article Text:
MEA urges Venezuela to look into the death of Indian sailor whose organs were alleg

Family alleges murder, organ removal

Union flags irregularities, missing organs

NEW DELHI: India has urgently requested Venezuelan authorities to investigate the alleged desecration of an Indian sailor's body after his family claimed all vital organs were removed before his mortal remains were repatriated."We have taken up the matter with Venezuelan authorities with a request to urgently investigate the matter," Ministry of External Affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said on Friday.The Indian Embassy in Caracas has sought a thorough investigation into the desecration and removal of organs from the mortal remains of Indian sailor Rakesh Chauhan. "Mission has continued to pursue this matter with the concerned local authorities since the incident came to light," the embassy said in a statement.Chauhan's wife, Ranjana, alleged that her husband died under suspicious circumstances while working aboard a vessel in Venezuela, with his organs removed before the body was returned to India."My husband had gone to work on a ship; he was murdered there, and his organs were removed. To this day, they haven't given us a proper report, despite our repeated requests," she told ANI.She said the family decided to conduct a second post-mortem after the body reached India."When we had a second post-mortem done here, the report revealed that not a single organ remained in the body... I last spoke to him on 6 May. It was only two or three hours after he left for duty that my father-in-law received a call saying there had been an accident," she said.The Forward Seamen's Union of India (FSUI) claimed the post-mortem conducted in India found no internal organs in Chauhan's body. According to the union, the brain, heart, both lungs, kidneys, liver, spleen, pancreas, stomach, intestines, thyroid, hyoid bone, larynx and trachea were all reportedly missing.The union also alleged irregularities in the documentation, claiming the receipt for mortal remains was forged — signed in the name of "Anjana Chauraisya" instead of "Ranjana Chaurasiya" — and that the vessel name mentioned in the employment agreement did not match the one where he was actually posted.
Article Summary: India has urgently requested Venezuelan authorities to investigate the alleged desecration of an Indian sailor's body. His family claimed all vital organs were removed before his mortal remains were repatriated. The Forward Seamen's Union of India (FSUI) claimed the post-mortem conducted in India found no internal organs in the body.

Article Title: What makes a 24-hour restaurant more than just an all-day café?
Article Text:
Zolocrust

There is a confidence that some places carry without effort. Not the confidence of the new or the loud, but something quieter and harder to manufacture. The kind that comes from having nothing to conceal and no particular interest in concealing it. At Zolocrust, this confidence is built into the walls. The kitchen is open in its entirety. Every station, every chef, every detail of what is being prepared is visible from the moment you walk in. You are not being given a curated view of the cooking. You are seeing all of it.Zolocrust, which operates across both cities and around the clock, has become that place for a particular kind of guest. One who has stopped separating the question of where to eat from the question of how they want to feel. The day crowd arrives with purpose. The night crowd arrives with time. The connection is not incidental. That is the entire point.The menu is entirely vegetarian, with a wide selection of vegan and gluten-free dishes. A significant portion of the ingredients used at the Jaipur restaurant, as well as its newer outpost at Clarks Avadh in Lucknow, is sourced from four on-site gardens spanning more than 70 beds. The gardens cultivate a variety of produce, including basil, broccoli, asparagus, bok choy, chillies and carrots.This farm-to-table approach reflects a broader shift in consumer preferences towards fresher, traceable ingredients and greater transparency in food sourcing. By growing a portion of its produce on-site, the restaurant shortens the journey from harvest to plate, allowing seasonal ingredients to be incorporated into the menu soon after they are picked.The new additions to the menu reflect the restaurant's broader focus on fresh, ingredient-led cooking. Among them is a poke bowl featuring seasoned sushi rice, silken tofu, avocado, edamame, cucumber and togarashi, offering a lighter option alongside the existing menu. The restaurant has also introduced Neapolitan-style pizzas with freshly stretched dough, while beverages such as the Orange & Basil Soda Pop expand the selection of house-made drinks. Together, these additions align with the growing demand for fresh, minimally processed and plant-based dining options.Summer evenings in North India are long and warm and full of people who stepped outside and found they were not ready to go back in. Zolocrust does not treat the night as a lesser version of the day. The kitchen runs identically. The menu does not shrink. The chefs on the night shift are not a reduced team.In Jaipur, this rhythm has been running for years. The guest who arrives at two in the morning is not a late guest. They are simply guests. The cup wall, where visitors have left their doodles on glasses across years of visits, holds its own quiet archive of people who came and felt comfortable enough to leave something behind.Meanwhile in Lucknow, it carries the same hours and the same intention. A city that already understands long evenings and conversations that do not end on schedule. Zolocrust does not adapt to that culture. It shares it. The kitchen is open. The garden is outside. Everything else follows from that.As diners increasingly seek experiences that combine transparency, quality and convenience, restaurants are adapting in ways that go beyond expanding their menus. At Zolocrust, the emphasis on open kitchens, locally grown produce and round-the-clock service reflects a broader shift in how restaurants are responding to evolving consumer expectations. Rather than focusing on a single offering, the concept brings together food, sourcing and accessibility in a way that mirrors changing dining habits.Disclaimer - The above content is non-editorial, and TIL hereby disclaims any and all warranties, expressed or implied, relating to it, and does not guarantee, vouch for or necessarily endorse any of the content.
Article Summary: Zolocrust operates across both Jaipur and Lucknow. A significant portion of the ingredients used at the restaurant are sourced from four on-site gardens. The farm-to-table approach reflects a broader shift in consumer preferences towards fresher, traceable ingredients.

Article Title: Argentina vs Cabo Verde FIFA World Cup match result: Argentina survive Cabo Verde scare in extra-time classic
Article Text:
Defending champions Argentina faced a fierce challenge but managed to edge out Cape Verde with a thrilling 3-2 win in extra time, earning their place in the World Cup Round of 16.(AP Photo)

Defending champions Argentina survived an almighty scare to secure a breathless 3-2 extra-time victory over debutants Cape Verde on Saturday, booking their spot in the World Cup Round of 16 after an epic battle that pushed the reigning kings to their absolute limits.The Blue Sharks, a tiny island nation off the West African coast, played out a script destined for folklore, refusing to buckle under the weight of the pro-Argentina crowd in South Florida. It took a cruel 111th-minute own-goal to finally shatter their resistance when a thumping Cristian Romero header deflected off Cape Verde’s Diney Borges into the net, sealing a pre quarter-final ticket for La Albiceleste.Earlier, it was the peerless Lionel Messi who ignited the stadium in the 29th minute. Sprinting behind the defense, Messi latched onto a lofted pass from Lisandro Martínez, took a sublime first touch, and hammered a clinical finish into the roof of the net. The strike marked Messi’s record-extending 20th career World Cup goal, catapulting him to the top of the Golden Boot race with seven goals this tournament, ahead of France’s Kylian Mbappé.Yet, Cape Verde’s fairy-tale run, anchored by a heroic 10-save performance from 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha was far from over.Deroy Duarte stunned the holders with a clever equalizer in regulation time. When extra-time commenced, Lisandro Martínez briefly restored Argentina’s lead in the 103rd minute, only for Sidny Lopes Cabral to answer back almost immediately with a stunning equalizer that left the stadium in utter disbelief.Ultimately, championship grit carried Argentina through the chaos, preserved by late goalkeeper heroics to deny a desperate Cape Verde surge in the final seconds.While the African debutants bow out after a historic campaign that included jaw-dropping group-stage results against Spain and Uruguay, Argentina march on. The world champions now head to Atlanta on Tuesday for a high-stakes Round of 16 clash against Egypt, who advanced after edging Australia in a penalty shootout.
Article Summary: Argentina beat Cape Verde 3-2 in extra time to reach World Cup Round of 16. Cristian Romero's header deflected off Diney Borges into the net in 111th minute. Lionel Messi opened the scoring in the 29th minute with a sublime finish.

Article Title: "Fraudulent team exposed": Fans slam Lionel Messi and Argentina as Cape Verde wins hearts in World Cup thriller
Article Text:
Fans slam Lionel Messi and Argentina after Cape Verde's heroic World Cup fight. (Image via: X)

Cape Verde pushed the defending champions to the edge in an unforgettable contest

Fans praise Cape Verde while criticizing Argentina after thrilling win

Argentina escaped one of the biggest scares of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after edging Cape Verde 3-2 in extra time in a dramatic Round of 32 clash. Lionel Messi scored another historic World Cup goal, but the defending champions were pushed to their limits by a fearless Cape Verde side that refused to back down.Even when Argentina booked its place in the Round of 16, the debate did not die down even after the last whistle. Fans instead flooded social media timelines with admiration for Cape Verde’s extraordinary performance, saying the tournament newcomers deserved respect worldwide despite the heart-breaking setback.Many expected Argentina to cruise into the next round after Messi opened the scoring with his record extending 20th World Cup goal. Instead, Cape Verde answered with incredible determination. Deroy Duarte brought his side level in the second half before Lisandro Martinez restored Argentina's lead early in extra time.Just when the match seemed over, Sidny Lopes Cabral produced a stunning long range strike that left fans in disbelief and tied the game again. Argentina finally secured the win after a corner kick ended in an own goal by Diney Borge, but Cape Verde continued attacking until the final whistle and nearly forced penalties.The result sent Argentina into the Round of 16, yet many viewers felt the scoreline told only part of the story. Cape Verde, playing in its first World Cup knockout match, matched one of football's biggest powers with courage, discipline, and belief for more than 120 minutes.Supporters praised goalkeeper Vozinha, who made several key saves against Messi and frustrated the defending champions throughout the night.One fan wrote, "100% successful dribbles. Vozinha owns Argentina and Messi." Another supporter backed Cape Verde's quality by saying, "From what I saw this Cape Verde team can beat Portugal any day."Several fans also pointed out how difficult Argentina found the contest. One post read, "Argentina needed extra time and 2 corner kicks to beat Cabo Verde" Many supporters believed the result mattered less than Cape Verde's performance. One fan wrote, "Argentina won the game but Cape Verde won our hearts."Others used the match to criticize the reigning champions. One viral reaction stated, "Super proud of them. They exposed a fraudulent team." Another emotional message highlighted how Cape Verde's inspiring run had changed global perceptions of the nation. The fan wrote, "The world is so proud of these players from Cape Verde and I believe more people across the world will definitely know this country now. They really shook the world of football and proved doubters wrong about them. Even Messi will never forget this particular match in his life."Another supporter summed up the mood across social media by writing, "Vozinha and Cape Verde deserved a standing ovation tonight." Argentina moves on with its World Cup dream still alive, but Cape Verde leaves the tournament with its reputation transformed. The tournament debutants earned admiration across the football world after producing one of the greatest underdog performances of the 2026 World Cup, proving they belonged on the sport's biggest stage.
Article Summary: Cape Verde pushed the defending champions to the edge in an unforgettable contest. Fans flooded social media timelines with admiration for Cape Verde’s extraordinary performance. The tournament debutants earned admiration across the football world after producing one of the greatest underdog performances of the 2026 World Cup.

Article Title: Jhon Arias strike sends Colombia into FIFA World Cup Round of 16 after hard-fought win over Ghana
Article Text:
Colombia's Jhon Arias (11) celebrates after scoring goal (AP Photo)

Jhon Arias' first-half goal proved decisive as Colombia defeated Ghana 1-0 on Friday night to secure a place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16, where they will face Switzerland for a spot in the quarter-finals.The South American side took control early despite suffering an injury setback within the opening minutes. Forward Jhon Córdoba was forced off after appearing to suffer a groin problem, prompting head coach Néstor Lorenzo to introduce Luis Suárez of Sporting CP. The substitution paid immediate dividends.In the 14th minute, Daniel Muñoz released Suárez down the flank, and the forward delivered a dangerous low cross into the box. Arias timed his run perfectly, guiding the ball beyond Ghana goalkeeper Lawrence Ati Zigi to hand Colombia the only goal of the contest.Colombia continued to dominate proceedings after the breakthrough and came close to extending their advantage. Luis Díaz thought he had doubled the lead midway through the second half, only for the offside flag to deny him. The winger was later thwarted by an excellent save from Ati Zigi, who finished the match with seven saves to keep Ghana within touching distance.Played in oppressive conditions at Arrowhead Stadium, where temperatures reached 31.1 degrees Celsius with a heat index of 96 degrees Fahrenheit at kick-off, the match tested both teams physically. Hydration breaks proved essential as several players battled cramps and signs of dehydration throughout the evening.Colombia entered the knockout stage in excellent form after navigating the group phase with victories over Uzbekistan and Congo, alongside a draw against Portugal, while conceding just one goal. Their performances had already attracted praise from Spain head coach Luis de la Fuente, who described Los Cafeteros as genuine contenders for the World Cup title.The Colombian supporters also turned the occasion into a home-like atmosphere, filling the stadium with yellow long before kick-off.Ghana, who had exceeded expectations by advancing from a challenging group featuring England and Croatia after missing out on the Africa Cup of Nations last year, struggled to create meaningful opportunities. Colombia's organised defence and quick transitions repeatedly frustrated the Black Stars, limiting them to eight attempts on goal, none of which required a save from the Colombian goalkeeper.The victory keeps Colombia's impressive World Cup campaign alive as they prepare for a last-16 showdown with Switzerland on Tuesday.
Article Summary: Colombia defeated Ghana 1-0 in the World Cup 2026 Round of 16. Jhon Arias scored the only goal of the game in the first half. Colombia will face Switzerland in the last 16 on Tuesday. The match was played in temperatures of 31.1 degrees Celsius.

Article Title: What is next for Vladimir Kramnik after chess legend handed two-year ban by FIDE?
Article Text:
Russian chess grandmaster Vladimir Kramnik (Ennio Leanza/Keystone via AP)

Why FIDE took action

Kramnik plans to appeal

ALSO READ:

Former World Chess Champion Vladimir Kramnik has been handed a two-year ban by the International Chess Federation (FIDE) after its Ethics & Disciplinary Commission found that he violated several rules related to player conduct and public accusations.The case centred on a series of statements and social media posts made by Kramnik about Czech grandmaster David Navara, Daniel Naroditsky, and other players.After reviewing evidence and submissions from all sides, the independent FIDE panel concluded that Kramnik's actions crossed several ethical boundaries.These included violations related to respecting the dignity of others, bullying and cyberbullying, psychological abuse, making unjustified public accusations, failing to cooperate with a fair play investigation, and not meeting the standards expected of a senior figure in chess.FIDE stressed that fighting cheating remains a top priority, but said that accusations must be handled through official and confidential channels and supported by solid evidence. The commission found that publicly linking identifiable players to cheating allegations without proper verification could seriously damage their reputation and mental well-being.The panel clarified that it was not judging whether Kramnik's anti-cheating methods were scientifically valid.Instead, it focused on how those allegations were made publicly and the impact they had on members of the chess community. Some allegations against Kramnik, including claims related to integrity and causing reputational damage to FIDE, were dismissed due to insufficient evidence.Kramnik received a two-year worldwide ban from FIDE events and official chess roles. However, the final year of the suspension has been suspended for a three-year probation period, meaning he will serve an active one-year ban if no further violations occur. He was also ordered to complete 12 months of unpaid service benefiting the chess community.Following the ruling, Kramnik posted on X:"Official Statement regarding EDC decision from 3 July 2026"I have just reviewed the text of the Ethics Commission’s decision in case 12/2025."I had already prepared in advance the text of my appeal, which I intend to file in the coming days. I will supplement it with new points regarding numerous procedural and other violations that I learned about from today’s document."Without going into details, the number of gross violations of major articles of the FIDE Regulations committed by the Ethics Commission in this case runs into the dozens."I am absolutely confident that this unlawful verdict will ultimately be overturned, and I intend to pursue all necessary instances to the very end in order to restore justice and, in particular, my reputation."Blatant and undisguised lawlessness must be — and will be — punished.Vladimir Kramnik"Kramnik has 21 days to file an official appeal against the decision.
Article Summary: Vladimir Kramnik handed a two-year ban by the International Chess Federation (FIDE) Ethics & Disciplinary Commission found that he violated several rules related to player conduct and public accusations. Case centred on a series of statements and social media posts.

Article Title: 'Remember the name Cape Verde': Ashwin lauds FIFA World Cup debutants
Article Text:
Messi shoots the ball during the match between Argentina and Cape Verde (Agency Image)

Former India off-spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has showered praise on FIFA World Cup debutants Cabo Verde after their remarkable campaign came to an end with a narrow 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina in the Round of 32.Although the African nation bowed out of the tournament, their performances throughout the competition earned admiration from fans and experts alike. Cabo Verde announced themselves on the world stage by holding European champions Spain to a goalless draw before securing draws against Saudi Arabia and former world champions Uruguay to reach the knockout phase in their maiden FIFA World Cup appearance.Their dream run almost continued against Lionel Messi 's Argentina. Cabo Verde twice fought back to level the scores and forced the reigning champions into extra time before an unfortunate own goal from Diney Borges in the 111th minute proved to be the difference.Impressed by the team's fearless performances, Ashwin took to X to applaud Cabo Verde and urged fans to remember the name of the tournament debutants."Whatever happens from here on in. Remember the name "CAPE VERDE" What a team and what a show they have put on so far this World Cup. #FIFAworldcup," posted the Indian cricket legend.Cabo Verde made a confident start against Argentina and threatened early through Ryan Mendes before Messi produced a moment of brilliance to break the deadlock in the 29th minute.The Argentine captain controlled a diagonal pass from Lisandro Martinez before blasting his finish into the roof of the net to register his seventh goal of the tournament.The underdogs responded strongly after the interval. Deroy Duarte finished clinically from a tight angle after being played through by Mendes, becoming the first Cabo Verde player to score in a FIFA World Cup knockout match.Argentina regained the lead at the start of extra time when Lisandro Martinez scored from close range following a corner, but Cabo Verde refused to surrender. Sidny Lopes Cabral curled home a superb equaliser in the 103rd minute to make it 2-2 and keep their hopes alive.The decisive blow came in the 111th minute when Messi's corner found Cristian Romero, whose header deflected off Diney Borges and into the net for the winning goal. Cabo Verde came close to forcing a penalty shootout in the closing stages, only for Emiliano Martinez to produce a crucial save from Lopes Cabral's powerful free-kick.Argentina have now advanced to the Round of 16, where they will take on Egypt, while Cabo Verde leave the tournament with their reputation greatly enhanced after one of the most memorable debut campaigns in FIFA World Cup history.
Article Summary: Ravichandran Ashwin hails Cabo Verde after their World Cup debut. Cape Verde lost 3-2 to Argentina in extra time in the Round of 32. Ashwin urges fans to remember the name of the tournament debutants.

If today's puzzle has you stumped, we've got you covered with spoiler-free hints, category names and the complete answers for NYT Connections puzzle #1115 for Tuesday, June 30, 2026.In NYT Connections, players are given a grid of 16 words and must organise them into four groups of four that share a common theme. The categories range from easy to difficult and are colour-coded by the game:Players can make up to four incorrect guesses before the game ends, making careful observation and logical thinking key to solving the puzzle.Want a nudge before checking the answers? Some players may have associated FENCE with Olympic sports, while BOX could have been mistaken for a sporting event rather than a recyclable item. The Purple category was the toughest of the day, requiring players to recognise that all four words represented different meanings of the word "draft" rather than belonging to a more obvious theme.
Article Summary: The New York Times' Connections puzzle has become a daily favourite for word game enthusiasts. Players are given a grid of 16 words and must organise them into four groups of four that share a common theme. The categories range from easy to difficult and are colour-coded by the game.

Article Title: England announce ODI squad for India series; James Coles, Josh Tongue earn maiden call-ups
Article Text:
England captain Harry Brook (AFP Photo)

James Coles, Josh Tongue earn first ODI call-ups



Jofra Archer returns



India to field strong squad



England ODI squad



NEW DELHI: England on Friday announced a 16-member squad for the three-match ODI series against India later this month, with uncapped all-rounder James Coles and fast bowler Josh Tongue earning their maiden call-ups.India and England are currently playing a five-match T20I series before locking horns in three ODIs on July 14, 16 and 19 in Birmingham, Cardiff and Lord's, respectively.The biggest talking points in England's squad are the inclusions of Coles and Tongue, who are yet to make their ODI debuts.The selectors have also included seamers Gus Atkinson and Saqib Mahmood as England prepare to face a strong Indian side.Fast bowler Jofra Archer has also been named in the squad and is set to return to ODI cricket for the first time since November 2025.His return will boost England's pace attack ahead of the three-match series.India are expected to field a near full-strength squad for the ODI series.Former captain Rohit Sharma and pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah are set to feature, while Virat Kohli 's participation will depend on his fitness.Harry Brook (captain), Tom Banton (wicketkeeper), Jacob Bethell, Joe Root, Ben Duckett, Jos Buttler (wicketkeeper), Will Jacks, Rehan Ahmed, Jofra Archer, Gus Atkinson, James Coles, Sam Curran, Liam Dawson, Saqib Mahmood, Adil Rashid, Josh Tongue.
Article Summary: England name 16-member squad for three-match ODI series against India. James Coles and Josh Tongue earn first ODI call-ups. Jofra Archer set to return to ODI cricket for the first time since November 2025.

Article Title: Egypt team staff member forcefully pushed by US police officer as fans gather outside team hotel ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026 R32 Australia clash
Article Text:
Mohamed Salah (via AP)

Chaos breaks out as Egypt team greets fans outside Dallas hotel

The build-up to Egypt’s FIFA World Cup Round of 32 match against Australia took an unexpected turn after a confrontation broke out between a member of the Egyptian delegation and a US police officer outside the team’s hotel in Dallas.The incident happened as players were meeting fans gathered outside the hotel for photos and autographs. A video shared on social media showed the situation quickly turning tense before security and team officials stepped in to separate those involved. No injuries or arrests were reported.Egypt’s arrival in Dallas was meant to be a celebration ahead of their knockout clash with Australia at AT&T Stadium. A large group of supporters, including many children, waited outside the team’s hotel hoping to meet their favorite players.Among those greeting fans was winger Trezeguet. He was seen signing a shirt and posing for a selfie with a young supporter. Moments later, Egypt team director Ibrahim Hassan walked over to the same area.The situation changed within seconds. A US police officer responsible for security approached Hassan. Video footage appears to show the officer pushing the former Egypt international backward while asking people to move away from the area.The confrontation quickly became physical. Several Egypt players and staff rushed over after seeing the incident. A brief struggle followed as both sides tried to control the situation. Throughout the exchange, the officer repeatedly instructed those around him to keep their distance and step back from the restricted area.The tense moment lasted for about half a minute. Other security personnel and members of the Egypt delegation then intervened. They separated everyone involved and escorted the upset Egyptian officials away from the entrance, bringing the confrontation to an end.Neither the Egyptian Football Association nor local authorities immediately released detailed statements about the incident. There were also no reports of arrests, injuries, or disciplinary action following the brief clash.Ibrahim Hassan is one of Egypt’s most respected former footballers. The 59-year-old played 131 international matches for his country, making him one of the nation’s most-capped players. His twin brother, Hossam Hassan, is Egypt’s head coach during the 2026 FIFA World Cup and is widely regarded as one of the country’s greatest footballers.Despite the off-field distraction, Egypt’s attention remains firmly on its historic World Cup campaign. The team reached the Round of 32 after finishing second in Group G. They opened with a draw against Belgium, followed it with a victory over New Zealand, and secured qualification with another draw against Iran.Egypt had never won a World Cup match before this tournament. Captain Mohamed Salah has been central to Egypt’s success. The experienced forward has scored one goal and provided two assists in the group stage. Egypt will now look to their star player once again as they prepare to face Australia for a place in the last 16.
Article Summary: Chaos breaks out as Egypt team greets fans outside Dallas hotel. Video footage appears to show the officer pushing the former Egypt international backward while asking people to move away from the area. Several Egypt players and staff rushed over after seeing the incident. No injuries or arrests were reported.

Article Title: 'This is India's moment': Why the nation of 1.4 billion now has a dedicated pathway to Esports World Cup
Article Text:
Aravindh Chithambaram, Arjun Erigaisi, and Nihal Sarin (Special Arrangements)

India Rising: Road to EWC 2026 (Photo from Chess.com)

READ ALSO:

NEW DELHI: The traditional boundaries of Indian sports are expanding rapidly into the digital realm, with the India Rising: Road to EWC, a collaboration between JioBLAST, Chess.com, and the Esports World Cup (EWC) Foundation, emerging as the latest expression of the country's soaring esports ambitions.Through the India Rising event, India has secured a permanent qualification pathway on the official global Esports World Cup roadmap, with Indian Grandmaster Vidit Gujrathi facing Dutch Grandmaster Benjamin Bok in the summit clash on Saturday for a coveted spot in the 14-player Esports Chess World Cup roster.The choice to use chess as the initial catalyst for this integration is entirely deliberate.Indian chess is currently experiencing an unprecedented golden era, a competitive reality that Grandmaster Vidit believes makes the country the ideal starting point for a major global esports gateway."India is a powerhouse when it comes to chess. We have won the Olympiad gold medal. We are the current champions. The World Champion is from India, the Women's World Cup winner is from India, and challenger Vaishali is also from India," Gujrathi told TimesofIndia.com."So India is very important in the chess landscape. With the sheer number of players as well, I think India is one of the top places. I feel the competition is much higher here, and it makes a lot of sense that we have our own pathway to the Esports World Cup.As esports is such a global event, it's very important to have Indian representation there. I think we are among the favourites in the chess world, so it makes sense that we have this event for India. "Charlie Cowdrey, CEO of JioBLAST, reveals that putting India on the global EWC roadmap was born out of a desire to create lasting value rather than a fleeting digital spectacle.“The early conversations weren't about simply bringing another tournament to India, they were about creating meaningful opportunities for Indian players," Cowdrey, when asked about their initial approach to creating a separate World Cup qualifier for India, told this website."Chess felt like the natural place to start because India has one of the strongest chess ecosystems in the world, with incredible talent, a passionate fanbase and a new generation that's redefining the sport globally.”This strategic roadmap integration arrives at a time when the domestic market has reached what Cowdrey terms a "perfect storm".India holds the second-largest gaming market by player base, energised by a massive demographic advantage where 65% of the population is under the age of 35. It is a scale that global stakeholders can no longer afford to overlook.Mike McCabe, COO & Deputy CEO of the Esports World Cup Foundation, points out that the international body is formally embedding India into its long-term blueprint because the country is actively driving the industry forward.“India is one of the most important growth markets in global esports. It combines enormous scale, a young and digitally connected population, world-class competitive talent, and a gaming community that continues to expand every year," McCabe observed. "We see India not simply as a future market, but as a market that is already shaping the future of esports. When you look at the growth of esports as a global sport, it is difficult to imagine it without India playing a major role in it. ”However, establishing an entirely new pipeline across multiple global organisations presented distinct behind-the-scenes hurdles.Cowdrey notes that aligning international partners required meticulous problem-solving to balance competitive integrity with mainstream entertainment.“Whenever you're building something that's never been done before, alignment becomes one of the biggest challenges," Cowdrey admitted. "We were working across multiple global partners, each bringing different expertise but united by the same ambition... Finding the right balance between competitive integrity, accessibility and entertainment required months of collaboration and planning with EWC, BLAST, Jio, Chess.com and more. ”The ultimate objective of this roadmap placement was to address a historical structural gap, the lack of a direct path for raw domestic talent to reach the highest international stages."The countries that succeed over the long term are the ones that build durable structures around players rather than relying on short-term momentum," McCabe explained. "India Rising creates something that every developing esports ecosystem needs: a credible pathway from grassroots competition to the global stage.”With JioBLAST recently signing an MoU with the Government of Maharashtra to explore long-term digital innovation and youth engagement, the roadmap is clearly built for the long haul.“The ultimate marker of success for India Rising: Road to EWC will be to see Indian players and teams winning on the global Esports World Cup stage in the years to come,” Cowdrey added.On Saturday, a victory over Bok could make Gujrathi the fourth Indian to qualify for the EWC main event after Aravindh Chithambaram, Nihal Sarin, and Arjun Erigaisi, and it would surely be the cherry on top for both Indian chess and esports fans alike.“This is India's moment," McCabe concluded. The country already has the talent, the passion, and the scale to become one of the defining forces in global esports.”
Article Summary: India Rising: Road to EWC is the latest expression of the country's soaring esports ambitions. Indian Grandmaster Vidit Gujrathi faces Dutch Grandmaster Benjamin Bok in the summit clash on Saturday for a coveted spot in the 14-player Esports Chess World Cup roster.

Article Title: 'How has Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not played?' : Ex-England captain baffled by India's decision
Article Text:
India's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (ANI Photo)

Poll Vote & Share your view Who should be dropped to accommodate Sooryavanshi in the starting XI? Abhishek Sharma Sanju Samson 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

The excitement around Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has gone far beyond India, with the 15-year-old attracting attention from cricket fans across the world. Such has been the hype surrounding the teenage sensation that tickets for India's T20Is in Ireland were snapped up by supporters hoping to watch him in action. However, Sooryavanshi remained on the sidelines throughout the two-match series.The story did not change after India travelled to Durham for the T20Is against England. Despite the growing anticipation around his international debut, the youngster once again had to settle for a place on the bench. His continued omission has left several former cricketers puzzled, including Ravi Shastri and Sunil Gavaskar . Now, former England captains Michael Vaughan and Alastair Cook have also questioned India's decision.Speaking on the Stick to Cricket podcast, Vaughan admitted he could not understand why Sooryavanshi had not been given an opportunity, even suggesting he would like to put the question directly to the BCCI and chairman of selectors Ajit Agarkar.“We must say to the BCCI and the selector, Ajit, how has he not played against Ireland? Our mate, the Kid,” said Vaughan on the Stick to Cricket podcast, to which Cook replied: “I’m there tomorrow, and I’m driving up as a fan to make sure that the 15-year-old kid plays.”While Vaughan and Cook's comments reflected the growing curiosity over Sooryavanshi's absence, the chances of the Indian team management changing course appear slim. The selectors and coaching staff have consistently maintained that the teenager's opportunity will come at the appropriate time.At present, Sooryavanshi's path into the playing XI is blocked by Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson , both of whom remain firmly established in India's T20I side. Having played key roles in India's third T20 World Cup triumph four months ago, neither batter is expected to lose his place anytime soon.Even so, Sooryavanshi's omission has continued to spark debate considering the extraordinary rise he has enjoyed over the past five months. The left-hander first captained India Under-19 to the World Cup title, smashing a breathtaking 175 in the final. He then carried that momentum into the IPL, piling up 776 runs to become the youngest-ever Orange Cap winner.Before joining the senior squad for the Ireland tour, Sooryavanshi also impressed for India A in Sri Lanka. His match-winning 94 in the final included the fastest half-century in List A cricket and played a decisive role in India's comprehensive victory, further strengthening the case of those eager to see the teenage prodigy make his international debut.
Article Summary: India's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is yet to make his international debut. The 15-year-old was left on the bench for India's T20Is against England. Michael Vaughan and Alastair Cook have questioned the selectors' decision. Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson remain firmly established in the side.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: Mumbai Metro Line 2A services affected by technical issue; trains running on single line between Dahanukarwadi and Dahisar East
Article Text:
According to Maha Mumbai Metro, trains are operating normally between Andheri West and Dahanukarwadi. (File photo used for representational purpose)





MUMBAI: Services on Metro Line 2A were affected on Saturday after a technical issue disrupted normal operations between Dahisar East and Kandarpada stations.According to Maha Mumbai Metro, trains are operating normally between Andheri West and Dahanukarwadi.However, between Dahanukarwadi and Dahisar East, metro services were being run on a single-line bi-directional system, with trains operating in both directions on one track under regulated movement.Metro authorities clarified that services have not been suspended and trains continue to operate across the entire corridor, although passengers may experience longer waiting times due to the temporary arrangement.Technical teams were deployed to restore normal two-line operations at the earliest.The services were restored soon after.
Article Summary: Services on Metro Line 2A were affected on Saturday after a technical issue disrupted normal operations between Dahisar East and Kandarpada stations. According to Maha Mumbai Metro, trains are operating normally between Andheri West and Dahanukarwadi. Metro authorities clarified that services have not been suspended and trains continue to operate.

Article Title: Two die in Malegaon in slab collapse after heavy rain; landslides affect traffic movement in ghats
Article Text:
Amidst ongoing incessant rains received in Kolhapur and Satara districts, Friday turned out to be the day of landslides. Traffic disruptions occurred in Amba ghat and Karul ghat connecting Kolhapur and Ratnagiri districts owing to landslides. Traffic movement through both Karul ghat and Amba ghat has been diverted via Anuskura ghat as the works to remove the landslide debris are ongoing by the Public Works Department.

Nashik/Kolhapur:





A 65-year-old woman and her grandson were killed after a slab of their house collapsed in Malegaon during the first heavy spell of rain in the textile city, while incessant rainfall in Kolhapur and Satara districts led to multiple landslides and traffic disruptions on Friday.The deceased, Vimalbai Yewale (65) and Chetan Yewale (21), and another family member, Pranita Yewale, were inside the house in the city’s Collector Patta on Friday afternoon, when it started raining heavily.All three sustained severe injuries when the slab crashed. Local residents rescued them and took them to a private hospital, where Vimalbai and Chetan succumbed to their injuries during treatment. Pranita’s condition is critical. Inspector Subhash Sanap of the Chhavani police station said they have registered a case of accidental death.After landslides in Amba and Karul ghats, which connect Kolhapur and Ratnagiri districts, traffic was diverted via Anuskura ghat while the public works department removed debris. Minor landslides also occurred at Pasarni ghat, connecting Panchgani with Wai town, and in Ambenali ghat, connecting Mahabaleshwar in Satara district with Poladpur in Raigad district.A huge tree fell over the road at Amboli ghat, which connects Kolhapur and Sindhudurg districts, leading to a traffic blockade for nearly two hours on Friday afternoon.In Amba ghat, between Dakkhan and Kalakdara in Sangameshwar taluka of Ratnagiri district, a large landslide occurred on Friday morning around 9am. The iron crash barrier erected in the middle of the four-lane highway stopped a large boulder, preventing it from falling directly on moving vehicles or the road, and a major accident was averted.Passengers travelling through the ghat were asked to keep full control over vehicle speed, exercise special caution during the journey, and strictly follow instructions from the administration.Traffic movement between Gadhinglaj and Chandgad tehsils in Kolhapur district was disrupted after a service road bridge collapsed due to increased flow in the Hiranyakeshi river. Seven barrages in the district were submerged under waters of the Panchaganga, Bhogawati and Kasari rivers, affecting local traffic movement between villages. The Rajaram barrage over the Panchaganga river, adjacent to Kolhapur city, was also submerged on Friday at 7pm.Kolhapur district disaster management head, Prasad Sankpal, said, “Incessant rains have been received in the last two to three days. As per the India Meteorological Department, heavy rains are expected in the next three days in ghat areas and in plains from Kolhapur district.”While noting that the administration, farmers as well as citizens were delighted with the arrival of rains after a delay, he issued a word of caution. “The river waters from the district are rising. Five barrages from the district have been submerged, and orders have been given to authorities concerned to stop the traffic movement through these barrages. Even six inches of water has enough current to sweep a bigger vehicle like a truck. No one should take an extra risk and enter swollen waters. If any calamity takes place, then one can immediately contact the Disaster Management team by dialling the toll-free number ‘1077’,” Sankpal said.Heavy rains continued in the region, especially in Kolhapur and Satara districts. In Satara district, Mahabaleshwar received 158mm of rainfall, while Navaja received 105mm and Koyna 53mm in the 24 hours till 8.30am on Friday. Five dam catchments in Kolhapur district received above 95mm rainfall in those 24 hours.An ox belonging to farmer Amol Baburao Kognole was swept away into the flowing Panchaganga river water on Thursday evening in Waliwade village. Despite rescue efforts, the ox was not traced till Friday evening.
Article Summary: A 65-year-old woman and her grandson were killed after a slab of their house collapsed in Malegaon during the first heavy spell of rain in the textile city. Heavy rains in Kolhapur and Satara districts led to multiple landslides and traffic disruptions on Friday. In Satara district, Mahabaleshwar received 158mm of rainfall, while Navaja received 53mm.

Article Title: Teen and sibling thrashed after spat on train in Navi Mumbai
Article Text:
Another incident of verbal spat between a 16-year-old teenage boy and a 20-year-old youth took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night, after the youth accidentally stamped on the teen's foot. (File photo used for representational purpose)





NAVI MUMBAI: A day after the Borivli train murder incident following a violent altercation between two commuters that took place on June 23, yet another incident of verbal spat between a 16-year-old teenage boy and a 20-year-old youth took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night, after the youth accidentally stamped on the teen's foot.The altercation later spilled on to the street, when the youth accompanied by his two friends waylaid the teen near Seawoods Grand Central Mall around 9. 30pm and assaulted him with kicks and fist blows.As the teen's elder brother intervened to rescue him, the trio assaulted him as well in which he sustained a hand fracture.The injured brothers were identified as Rony Rana Shaikh (22) and Alkas Rana Shaikh (16).While, the accused youth was identified as Rohit Surendra Bhosale (22), all residing in Tata Nagar slum pocket, sector 10, CBD-Belapur near Konkan Bhawan.Accused Rohit Bhosale and his two unidentified aides were booked under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) sections for voluntarily causing grievous hurt and joint criminal liability.
Article Summary: A day after the Borivli train murder incident following a violent altercation between two commuters that took place on June 23, yet another incident of verbal spat took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night. The altercation later spilled on to the street, when the youth accompanied by his two friends waylaid the teen and assaulted him with kicks and fist blows.

Article Title: 4 youngsters trapped in elevator of Navi Mumbai high-rise for 90 minutes; rescued safely by firemen
Article Text: NAVI MUMBAI: Four youngsters were trapped inside an elevator between the third and fourth floors of Tulip CHS in Sector 34B, Kharghar, for about 90 minutes from 3 am on Friday due to a power outage in the residential area.The Kharghar fire brigade received the emergency call at 4.25 am and reached the society at 4.35 am.Firefighters rescued the trapped individuals — two men and two women, all aged between 20 and 22 — by forcing open the elevator door from outside using a crowbar and a battery-operated spreader tool.Prashant Darekar, a leading fireman at Kharghar, said,"It took us less than 5 minutes to rescue the trapped youngsters. While, one woman was a tenant in the building, the other three were her friends who had come to drop her home after they returned from a night out."
Article Summary: Four youngsters were trapped inside an elevator between the third and fourth floors of Tulip CHS in Sector 34B, Kharghar, for about 90 minutes from 3 am on Friday due to a power outage. Firefighters rescued the trapped individuals — two men and two women, all aged between 20 and 22 — by forcing open the elevator door from outside.

Article Title: Tree falls on man in Thane; 11 vehicles damaged
Article Text:
A 52-year-old man was injured when a tree unexpectedly fell on him in Thane's Wagle Estate, also damaging eleven parked vehicles





THANE: A 52-year-old man sustained head and chest injuries after a tree suddenly collapsed on him near Wagle Police Station, Tuesday morning. The fallen tree also damaged 11 parked vehicles, including a police bomb squad bus.This is the first tree fall injury reported this season raising questions over the monsoon preparedness of the Corporation.The incident was reported around 9am near Prashant Corner on Road No 31 in Wagle Estate."The pedestrian, identified as Pawan Jha, 52, a resident of Sai Dham Society near Pipeline, Dnyaneshwar Nagar, was on his way to work when he was struck by the falling tree. He suffered injuries to his head and chest and was admitted to Pragati Hospital in Wagle Estate for treatment," informed Yasin Tadvi, incharge of RDMC, Thane.The damaged vehicles include a Maharashtra Police Bomb Squad bus attached to Wagle Police Station, a light commercial vehicle, two cars, one autorickshaw, and six two-wheelers.Personnel from Wagle Police Station, the Tree Authority Department, the Disaster Management Cell, and the Fire Brigade rushed to the spot with rescue, fire, and pickup vehicles. Authorities removed the debris and cleared the affected area.
Article Summary: A 52-year-old man sustained head and chest injuries after a tree suddenly collapsed on him near Wagle Police Station, Tuesday morning. The fallen tree also damaged 11 parked vehicles, including a police bomb squad bus. This is the first tree fall injury reported this season raising questions over the monsoon preparedness of the Corporation.

Article Title: 'We were fortunate': Roberto Martinez defends VAR review; Croatia coach laments 'bad refereeing'
Article Text:
Portugal coach Roberto Martinez

Portugal head coach Roberto Martinez defended the Video Assistant Referee (VAR) decisions that shaped his side's dramatic 2-1 victory over Croatia in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32, while Croatia manager Zlatko Dalic criticised the officiating but refused to blame it entirely for his team's exit.Portugal booked their place in the Round of 16 after coming from behind to beat Croatia in Toronto, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring from the penalty spot before substitute Goncalo Ramos headed home a stoppage-time winner.The match was overshadowed by a series of VAR interventions, including the disallowing of two Croatian goals for offside.Speaking after the match, Martinez rejected suggestions that Croatia had been unfairly denied a dramatic late equaliser."There were no bad decisions. Today we were fortunate," Martinez said when asked if Croatia had been robbed of a second goal by VAR."The chip ball showed that it was offside, the penalty was also clear. I understand the work of (Croatia coach Zlatko) Dalic in this team and it is a shame that there was only one winner today," he added.The Portugal coach also looked ahead to his team's Round of 16 clash against Spain, predicting a high-quality encounter."We respect the quality of Spain. I think it is going to be a fantastic match, it is going to be the European game of this World Cup," Martinez said.Reflecting on the dramatic nature of the contest, he added: "I have lost my hair through this, but I think it is worth it."Dalic, meanwhile, expressed frustration with the officiating, believing Croatia were denied important decisions during the match."It was very bad refereeing," Dalic said, adding that Croatia "should have been given more free kicks."However, the Croatia manager stopped short of using the refereeing as an excuse for his side's elimination."But Croatia lost. I'm not going to find any excuses," he said. "We could have won this earlier."Croatia had taken the lead through Ivan Perisic, who became the country's all-time leading World Cup scorer, before Ronaldo's penalty brought Portugal level. Ramos' late winner ultimately sealed Portugal's progress and potentially brought an end to Luka Modric 's World Cup career.
Article Summary: Portugal beat Croatia 2-1 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32. Cristiano Ronaldo scored a penalty and Goncalo Ramos headed home a stoppage-time winner. Portugal coach Roberto Martinez defended the Video Assistant Referee (VAR) decisions. Croatia manager Zlatko Dalic criticised the officiating but refused to blame it entirely for his team's exit.

Article Title: Weekly special train services running via Coimbatore extended
Article Text:
Representative image





COIMBATORE: The service period of weekly special trains operated between Bengaluru-Thiruvananthapuram has been extended till July 30.According to a press release, No. 06547 SMVT Bengaluru-Thiruvananthapuram North weekly special train will leave SMVT Bengaluru at 7.25pm on July 1, 15, 22 and 29 (Wednesdays) to reach Thiruvananthapuram North at 1.15pm the next day.No. 06548 Thiruvananthapuram North-SMVT Bengaluru weekly special train will leave Thiruvananthapuram North at 3.15pm on July 2, 16, 23 and 30 (Thursdays) to reach SMVT Bengaluru at 8.30 am the next day.These trains will have stoppages at Krishnarajapuram, Bangarapet, Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Podanur, Palakkad, Thrissur, Aluva, Ernakulam Town, Kottayam, Changanassery, Thiruvalla, Chengannur, Mavelikara, Kayamkulam, Sasthankotta, Kollam and Varkala.
Article Summary: The service period of weekly special trains operated between Bengaluru- Thiruvananthapuram has been extended till July 30. The trains will have stoppages at Krishnarajpuram, Bangarapet, Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Podanur, Palakkad, Thrissur and Varkala.

Article Title: Three arrested with 4.3kg ganja in Coimbatore
Article Text:
Prohibition enforcement wing police in Coimbatore city on Tuesday arrested three men for smuggling ganja in a car near Sungam Junction

Coimbatore:





The prohibition enforcement wing (PEW) on Tuesday arrested three people for peddling ganja in a car near Sungam Junction in the city.The arrested were identified as P Manimaran, 28, of Podanur ; K Navarathinavel, 39, of Sundarapuram ; and D Harishankar, 28, of Sungam.They were apprehended after a team led by PEW inspector Miadit Mano conducted a vehicle check at Sungam Junction, following a tip off. Police intercepted a white car in which the three accused were travelling and found 4.3kg of ganja in the car.Police seized the contraband, along with the car and a two-wheeler used by the accused. All three were later produced before a court and remanded in judicial custody.
Article Summary: The prohibition enforcement wing (PEW) on Tuesday arrested three people for peddling ganja in a car near Sungam Junction in the city. The arrested were identified as P Manimaran, 28, of Podanur ; K Navarathinavel, 39, of Sundarapuram ; and D Harishankar, 28. All three were later produced before a court and remanded in judicial custody.

Article Title: NEET: Salem division to run special trains, announces helplines
Article Text:
Representative image





COIMBATORE: The Salem division of Southern Railway has made special arrangements to facilitate the candidates appearing for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET-UG) on Sunday.There are seven towns in the jurisdiction of Salem division where NEET will be conducted. They are Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Coimbatore, Ooty , Namakkal and Karur. Special arrangements have been made across the division, including operation of special trains, to facilitate smooth movement of candidates.Two special trains – one from Chinna Salem to Salem Jn and the other from Tirupattur to Salem Jn – will be operated on Sunday to facilitate candidates appearing for the NEET.Traffic at important level crossing gates will be monitored and regulated to avoid road congestion and ensure smooth movement of candidates to examination centres.Helpdesks will be available at Salem Jn, Erode Jn, Tirupur, Coimbatore Jn, Udagamandalam, Namakkal and Karur Jn railway stations to provide travel related information and assistance to candidates.The helplines: Salem Jn 8778998561, Erode Jn 9840575966, Tiruppur 7904724532, Coimbatore Jn 9384863048, Ooty 7338946729, Namakkal9443516702 and Karur Jn 8056256965.
Article Summary: There are seven towns in the jurisdiction of Salem division where NEET will be conducted. They are Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Coimbatore, Ooty, Namakkal and Karur. Special arrangements have been made across the division.

Article Title: Boy drowns in floodwaters near Erode
Article Text:
Representative image





Erode: A six-year-old boy drowned after being swept away by floodwaters in a stream near Thalavadi in Erode district on Sunday evening. The body of Madhan Karthik, son of Rangaraj of Diginarai village, was recovered on Monday evening. He was a Class II student.Following heavy rain in the Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve, the stream in Diginarai village was overflowing on Sunday evening.Karthik went to see the floodwaters after telling his parents, but he did not return home.His parents and relatives searched for him near the stream, but they did not find him. It later emerged that the boy was swept away by the floodwaters. They informed the Thalavadi police, who rushed to the spot and joined searches for the boy.Later, fire and rescue services personnel from Hasanur also reached the scene and joined the searches. They retrieved the boy's body from the stream close to Swarnavathi river in Chamarajanagar in Karnataka on Monday. The river is the tributary of the Cauvery. The body was sent to the Sathyamangalam government hospital for postmortem.A case was registered.
Article Summary: The body of Madhan Karthik, son of Rangaraj of Diginarai village, was recovered on Monday evening. He was a Class II student. Following heavy rain in the Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve, the stream was overflowing on Sunday evening.

Article Title: Kochi News - The Times of India
Article Text: By: Anannya RajeshOnce reserved for rare blockbusters, sequels are becoming a regular feature of Malayalam cinemaAs ‘Drishyam 3’ wraps up a successful theatrical run and begins its streaming journey, its success raises an obvious question: Are sequels becoming a fixture of Malayalam cinema rather than an occasional exception?For most of the industry’s history, they weren’t. Follow-ups were few and far between, reserved only for the rare breakout hit.The clearest proof of that shift isn’t ‘Drishyam’ at all; it is ‘Vaazha’. Nobody set out to build a franchise when the film went into production. It was a modestly budgeted title, cast almost entirely with newcomers, with no star name and no built-in audience.Yet it succeeded well enough to earn itself something no one had planned for: A sequel. ‘Vaazha 2’ followed and did even better than the first. That order of events matters: Newcomers, no built-in audience, and a franchise that only took shape once the film had already worked on its own.It runs against an idea the industry has held onto for years, that you need star power to launch anything worth continuing.“When a movie like that becomes a success, the confidence that we get is that instead of using stars, we can do a second part with newcomers,” Harris Desom , producer of the ‘Vaazha’ franchise, says.Four sequels came out in 2026 alone: ‘Drishyam 3’, ‘Aadu 3’, ‘Vaazha 2’, and ‘Mohiniyattam’, the follow-up to ‘Bharatanatyam’.That is a lot for an industry that used to treat original stories as the default. Whether this counts as a full-blown ‘sequel era’ is debatable, but the pattern is hard to miss.Part of the explanation is streaming. Actor Saiju Kurup, who worked on both the ‘Aadu’ films and the ‘Bharatanatyam’ and ‘Mohiniyattam’ series, points to ‘Aadu’ as an early case. It flopped at the box office, then found an audience later through DVDs, torrents, and digital platforms.That slow-building popularity eventually led to ‘Aadu 2’, and then ‘Aadu 3’. ‘Bharatanatyam’ had a similar afterlife; its streaming numbers were strong enough to convince makers that ‘Mohiniyattam’ was worth making.What both films show is that a franchise no longer has to prove itself in one opening weekend.A modest release can find its audience much later, on a platform, and that delayed audience is now enough to justify a sequel. The success of newcomer-led ‘Vaazha’ and the slow climb of ‘Aadu’ are really two versions of the same thing: Content earning its audience on its own schedule, without a star doing the work up front.That doesn’t mean sequels are a safer bet, Desom says. “Risk factor is never low. Cinema is always accompanied by its risk factors.” If anything, success raises the pressure with every instalment. “Vaazha 1’ was successful, ‘Vaazha 2’ was an industry hit,” he says. “When ‘Vaazha 3’ comes, we have that baggage with us.”Risk factors are never low, and a sequel carries almost the same risk as the first film, says Kurup. “To sustain its box-office run, the film has to entertain. There is no guarantee a sequel will succeed simply because the first film was a hit.” What gives a sequel its initial advantage, however, is not the story but the audience's attachment to its characters, he adds.“The nostalgia associated with sequels can be attributed not to the story but to its characters,” Kurup says. Audiences come back because they want more time with people they already know, not necessarily the same story again. That familiarity gives a sequel a headstart too: “The second part does not need time for character establishment because people are used to seeing them and they have liked the characters.”Filmmaker Rosshan Andrrews takes a more guarded view. He agrees sequels usually follow a hit, “sequels are almost always made when a film becomes a great success,” he says, and doesn’t shy from the commercial logic either.“From a business angle, it is perfect,” he says, pointing to how a hit strengthens theatrical, satellite, streaming and overseas prospects for what follows. Still, he doubts sequels often beat their originals, citing ‘The Godfather’, ‘The Terminator,’, and the recent ‘Dhurandhar 2’ as proof that a great sequel is genuinely rare. Having directed remakes himself, he knows how hard it is to revisit familiar material without it feeling limiting.Desom insists ‘Vaazha 2’ was a creative call before it was a business one. “We had a better script for the second than we did for the first,” he says. Kurup doesn’t separate the two: “Creative evolution happens because of market response.If the first part did not get a good response, no one would think creatively about extending it.” Nobody involved thinks a sequel takes anything away from the film it follows. The original still stands on its own; the sequel gets judged separately, by the same audience that liked the first one.A recognizable name might get people through the door, but as Desom puts it, “content takes precedence over brand value.”Malayalam cinema is clearly building more franchises than it used to. What hasn’t changed is what decides whether they last: Not the title, not the nostalgia, just whether the film itself is good enough to watch. If it is, audiences show up again. If it isn't, no franchise name will save it.
Article Summary: Four sequels came out in 2026 alone: 'Drishyam 3', 'Aadu 3' and 'Vaazha 2' Malayalam cinema used to treat original stories as the default. Part of the explanation is streaming. A modest release can find its audience much later.
