Article Title: Who is Valeria Rodriguez? Streamer and Voice actor's past controversies resurface after Cyberpunk: Edgerunners 2 casting
Article Text:
Valeria Rodriguez, known for roles in Overwatch 2, Genshin Impact, and Ranma ½, has been cast as Roman Carax in Cyberpunk: Edgerunners 2. The announcement revived debate over past social media controversies, including allegations about disputes involving fellow voice actors and confrontational online posts. While critics questioned the casting, supporters defended Rodriguez's work. The allegations circulating online remain unverified, and neither CD Projekt Red nor Studio Trigger has publicly addressed the backlash.

Valeria Rodriguez's Cyberpunk: Edgerunners 2 casting revives old controversies

Valeria Rodriguez's social media posts become a fresh talking point

Fans of anime and gaming are thrilled with the news that Valeria Rodriguez has joined Cyberpunk: Edgerunners 2. Old online controversies surrounding the voice actor resurfaced shortly after the casting announcement, igniting a social media debate. While many applauded Rodriguez’s latest role, others questioned the decision by pointing to past online disputes and allegations surrounding previous projects.Valeria Rodriguez has built a growing reputation in the voice acting industry, lending their voice to characters such as Venture in Overwatch 2, Sucrose in Genshin Impact, Akane Tendo in the Ranma ½ remake and Lagoona Blue in Monster High. They also maintain an active online presence through YouTube and X.Their latest career milestone came with the announcement that they will voice Roman Carax in Cyberpunk: Edgerunners 2. Rodriguez shared the news with fans on X, writing:"In case you missed it, choom- The chrome’s outta the ripperdoc, I’m voicing Roman Carax in Cyberpunk @edgerunners Season 2! Keeping this secret has been so hard and I am BEYOND excited for you all to see what we’ve been cooking. You’re not ready for this season…"However, the announcement also brought renewed attention to past controversies.An X user, industry reporter @AI_EmeraldApple, criticized the casting and alleged that Rodriguez had previously been removed as the English voice of Sucrose in Genshin Impact following public disputes. Those claims have circulated widely online, but they have not been independently verified.The same post also connected the controversy to fellow voice actor Courtney Lin, claiming Rodriguez publicly defended Lin after she reportedly lost work following participation in a boycott related to Genshin Impact. These allegations likewise remain unverified.Much of the criticism has centered on Rodriguez's past activity on X. Screenshots widely shared online appear to show the voice actor responding aggressively to critics.One message attributed to Rodriguez read:"You're a *** loser who's going to die alone and I would bet $1000 US dollars that you are incredibly depressed and hate yourself. Get therapy or rot alone in your bedroom but leave nice people out of it, nerd. That or have the balls to say this **** in-person. Not behind a pfp."After the exchange gained attention, Rodriguez clarified in another post that the comment was not intended as a death threat, stating:"It seems like literacy doesn't exist anymore so I'd like to clarify- my response to that racist troll celebrating my friend getting fired was NOT about wishing d3ath on them. I'd never do that. I said that they would die ! ALONE! AND I'D SAY IT AGAIN!"The re-emerged posts have split online communities. Some argue that a person’s social media presence should have no bearing on their professional work, while others believe that public figures should be held accountable for their online behavior.Neither CD Projekt Red nor Studio Trigger have issued an official comment responding to the online criticism, as the Cyberpunk: Edgerunners 2 announcement continues to generate discussion. The argument is still mostly limited to social media, where opinions continue to tussle over Rodriguez’s casting and past online activities.
Article Summary: Valeria Rodriguez, known for roles in Overwatch 2, Genshin Impact, and Ranma ½, has been cast as Roman Carax in Cyberpunk: Edgerunners 2. Announcement revived debate over past social media controversies, including allegations about disputes involving fellow voice actors.

Article Title: 'The press would go wild': Donald Trump suggests inviting Obama, Biden to football watch party
Article Text:
Trump jokes about inviting Obama, Biden for football watch party

US President Donald Trump joked about hosting an unprecedented gathering of America's living former presidents, suggesting they could reunite at the White House to watch a football game together.The remark came during an episode of Storytime with the Second Lady, a YouTube podcast hosted by US Second Lady Usha Vance. Released on Friday ahead of the 250th anniversary of American Independence, the episode featured Trump reading President's Play!, a children's book published by the White House Historical Association that explores how US presidents spent their leisure time.As Trump reached a page showing a president hosting a Super Bowl watch party at the White House, he proposed bringing together some of his predecessors despite years of public political clashes with several of them. "Maybe I should invite Barack Hussein Obama, Joe Biden , with the Bushes — or Bush. Maybe I should invite some of those people to watch a football game together," Trump said."Wouldn't that be a nice story? The press would go wild," he added.Vance responded, "A president reunion? That would be fun."The suggestion stood out given Trump's long-running attacks on former presidents Barack Obama and Joe Biden, whom he has regularly criticised at campaign rallies, public events and press conferences.The conversation also included Trump talking about serving in the White House. “You know, my attitude is you're at the White House for a short period of time and it's an honour to be here and you should work for the people, right?” Trump said.While reading through the book's illustrations of former commanders-in-chief, Trump paused at a page about Bill Clinton's fitness routine and unexpectedly spoke warmly about the former Democratic president. "That's nice. I didn't even know that until recently, but he had a track built at the White House," Trump said."I don't think I'll ever do that. I don't see myself doing that. I don't know. But he actually was a nice guy. I like Bill Clinton a lot. I still do," Trump said.Trump also offered a humorous take on William Howard Taft, recalling the former president's well-known love of baseball."He was a large man, very large. And he loved baseball. He'd go to baseball games, loved the hot dogs at the baseball games," he said."He was our heaviest president, and I have to be careful 'cause I don't want to supersede his record. And a thing like that would be possible if I allowed it to happen," Trump said.Further asked by Vance whether he gets time to read for pleasure as president, Trump said his reading largely revolves around current affairs."I usually read stories about myself," Trump said.As he continued reading the book, Trump shared his impressions of several former presidents, describing Lyndon Johnson as a "tough cookie," Ronald Reagan as a "high-quality person" who was "like your father was president," and John F. Kennedy as "the second-most good-looking president."Trump also used the opportunity to mention the ballroom under construction on the White House grounds.Read full story: Usha Vance faces backlash after inviting Trump to children's podcast The episode drew mixed reactions on social media, with some users mocking Trump's appearance on a children's reading podcast and questioning his reading habits after Vance remarked, "It's really kind of you to take the time to read."The podcast series features public figures reading children's books, with episodes released on YouTube by Vance.
Article Summary: US President Donald Trump joked about hosting an unprecedented gathering of America's living former presidents. The remark came during an episode of Storytime with the Second Lady, a YouTube podcast hosted by US Second Lady Usha Vance. The episode featured Trump reading President's Play!, a children's book that explores how US presidents spent their leisure time.

Article Title: Indian-origin Rep Thanedar tells 'racist' Brandon Gill what the Internet has been constantly reminding him. 'You married an Indian American'
Article Text:
Shri Thanedar picked a fight with Brandon Gill over the latter's hatred towards Indians.

Reactions to Thanedar picking a fight with Gill

Congressman Brandon Gill is known for his statements against Muslims and H-1B visa holders. Every time Gill slams the Indian people living in the US, the Internet reminds him that he married an Indian-American, Danielle D'Souza Gill, the daughter of MAGA commentator Dinesh D'Souza. This time, Indian-origin Rep Shri Thandar reminded Gill of this as Gill shared a quote that he said recently. "Calling us racist doesn't work anymore. The left has one debate tactic: call you a racist and pray you shut up. I never will," Gill said.Thanedar replied to Gill's quote and added that nobody is calling Brandon Gill a racist to shut him up. People call him racist because he called Indian Americans as "7-Eleven workers". Thanedar said. "But the fact that you married an Indian American shows that you're just a grifter pandering to a racist base. How pathetic it must be to be that cheap," Thanedar said and shared the screenshot of the social media post where Gill mentioned the '7-11 thing'7-Eleven is an American convenience store chain headquartered in Irving, Texas. A few months ago, Gill said it is idiotic that America is hiring 7-Eleven workers from India or from some other countries -- because these are not specialty occupations or skilled positions. His video was, in fact, a reaction to a YouTuber's video who showed an Indian person working in a 7-11 store in Texas."He's not calling Indians 7-11 workers. He's calling 7-11 workers 7-11 workers. We have literally given H1-B visas to 7-11 workers. That is what he is referring to. Obviously," one user wrote. Many X users commented that Thanedar did not understand Brandon's English and made a mistake, as Brandon did not call Indian-Americans 7-11 workers but he called out that 7-11 was hiring Indians and not Americans.
Article Summary: Congressman Brandon Gill is known for his statements against Muslims and H-1B visa holders. Every time Gill slams the Indian people living in the US, the Internet reminds him that he married an Indian-American, Danielle D'Souza Gill. This time, Indian-origin Rep Shri Thandar reminded Gill of this.
